Metadata.Case_Number: Originating Application No 1153 of 2024 (Registrar’s Appeal No 13 of 2025)
Metadata.Tribunal_Court: General Division of the High Court of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: Canstone Management Ltd

Facts.Text: Canstone Management Limited is a shareholder of Allestari Development Pte Ltd and holds 1.7% of the shares in Allestari according to the company register.
Canstone Management Limited was not a party to HC/OC 798/2023 concerning the relative shareholdings of Clarity China Partners LP and N Century Holding Company Limited.

Before commencing Originating Application No 1153 of 2024, Canstone Management Limited was aware of an interim injunction granted on 2022-11-25 in HC/OC 798/2023 that restrained changes to the composition of the board of Allestari Development Pte Ltd.
Canstone Management Limited filed Originating Application No 1153 of 2024 seeking court orders concerning the composition of the board of Allestari Development Pte Ltd.

In Originating Application No 1153 of 2024, Canstone Management Limited sought a declaration that three named individuals, Liu Xun, Guo Jian and Li Yi, are nominated directors of Allway Minerals and Science Technology Co., Ltd on the board of Allestari Development Pte Ltd.

In Originating Application No 1153 of 2024, Canstone Management Limited sought declarations that terms be implied into the memorandum and articles of association of Allestari Development Pte Ltd dated 2006-12-11 concerning the application of Article 76A and the allocation of rights to appoint directors among the majority, second largest and third largest shareholders.

In Originating Application No 1153 of 2024, Canstone Management Limited sought orders that Allestari Development Pte Ltd and its shareholders, directors and officers take reasonable steps to remove directors appointed by Allway Minerals and Science Technology Co., Ltd from the board and to fill any vacated seats in accordance with the terms it sought to have implied.
Li Yi was appointed as a director of Allestari Development Pte Ltd in 2008 and is described as the president and ultimate beneficial owner of Canstone Management Limited.

In 2018, Li Yi informed shareholders and directors of Allestari Development Pte Ltd that he would act in what he described as the fair interests of Allestari Development Pte Ltd and the minority shareholder Canstone Management Limited.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether, in light of Allestari Development Pte Ltd's existing constitution and an existing interim injunction preserving the board composition, Canstone Management Limited, as a 1.7% shareholder, could obtain court orders to imply terms into the constitution reallocating director appointment rights and to require steps to remove and replace certain directors.
Party_Details.Rule: Declaratory relief is generally granted where there is a real and present controversy between parties that the declaration will resolve. Terms may be implied into a company constitution or contract in fact where there is a gap that the parties did not contemplate, and implication is necessary to give the instrument business efficacy, with the implied term reflecting what the parties would have agreed at the time of contracting. Appointment and removal of directors are ordinarily governed by the company’s constitution and, where the constitution is silent, by default statutory provisions that allow appointment and removal by ordinary resolution.
Party_Details.Application: Canstone Management Limited, holding 1.7% of Allestari Development Pte Ltd, sought to alter how directors are appointed and removed by asking the court to imply new terms into the existing constitution and to order steps to remove three directors, including an individual who is also the president and ultimate beneficial owner of Canstone. The company’s constitution already contained provisions on nomination and appointment of directors, and statutory provisions and other articles provided mechanisms for appointment and removal by ordinary resolution. An interim injunction in separate proceedings preserved the existing board composition. Canstone was aware of that injunction when it filed its application. The relief sought would affect all shareholders and the existing board structure, while the mechanisms in the constitution and statute already provided ways for shareholders to change directors through corporate processes.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The judge dismissed Canstone Management Limited's appeal and upheld the assistant registrar's decision to strike out Originating Application No 1153 of 2024. The reasoning included that the application was an abuse of process because it operated as a collateral attack on an existing interim injunction in HC/OC 798/2023 that preserved the composition of Allestari Development Pte Ltd's board. The judge agreed that Canstone, being aware of the injunction, should have applied to vary it or sought leave before commencing proceedings that implicated the board composition. The judge further held that there was no live controversy justifying declaratory relief: it was undisputed that Allway Minerals and Science Technology Co., Ltd had appointed the three directors in 2008, and there was no evidence that Allway, after ceasing to be a shareholder in 2011, was asserting rights to appoint or remove directors or instructing the directors. The judge found that the claim to imply terms into Article 76A of the constitution was legally and factually unsustainable under the Sembcorp Marine framework. There was no necessary gap because the constitution and the Companies Act already provided mechanisms for appointment and removal of directors by ordinary resolution. The proposed implied term would effectively rewrite Article 76A by converting rights of named shareholders into rights of the majority, second largest and third largest shareholders, which the judge considered not to be a necessary implication but a reallocation of rights that should be decided by the members. The judge noted that shareholders, including Canstone, had allowed the three directors to remain on the board for more than a decade after Allway ceased to be a shareholder, and there was no basis to infer that, at the time of adopting the constitution, the shareholders would have agreed to the specific implied term sought. The judge also rejected the broader declaratory relief sought in prayer 4 as an incorrect general proposition about shareholders' ability to contract with non-shareholders regarding director nominations, and found no evidence of any such contractual arrangement in this case. Prayers seeking orders to remove the three directors and to fill vacancies in accordance with the proposed implied term were held to be unsustainable because the implied term itself was unsustainable and because there was no legal basis to compel shareholders to remove otherwise qualified directors. The judge concluded that the entire action should be struck out against all defendants, noting concerns about inconsistent outcomes and potential default judgments, and awarded costs of $12,000 all-in to N Century Holding Company Limited, explaining that preparation for an appeal requires counsel to be ready to address all issues afresh in a rehearing. The judge also observed that while Canstone framed its approach as seeking curial intervention for corporate governance, litigation should not be commenced without specific interests of the claimant at stake, and that the arguments advanced were vague, detached from any live controversy, and unsustainable.

Metadata.Case_Number: Originating Application No 1153 of 2024 (Registrar’s Appeal No 13 of 2025)
Metadata.Tribunal_Court: General Division of the High Court of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: Allestari Development Pte. Ltd.

Facts.Text: Allestari Development Pte Ltd is a company with multiple shareholders, including Canstone Management Limited, Clarity China Partners LP, N Century Holding Company Limited, Yunnan Nonferrous 308 Geological Exploration Team, China, Reliable Source Investments Limited, Brillante Resources Ltd and Allway Minerals and Science Technology Co., Ltd at different times.

According to the company register, Clarity China Partners LP holds 56.62% of the shares in Allestari Development Pte Ltd and N Century Holding Company Limited holds 20.28% of the shares, with the relative shareholdings of these two entities being the subject of dispute in HC/OC 798/2023.
The 4th to 6th defendants each hold less than 10% of the shares in Allestari Development Pte Ltd.
Allway Minerals and Science Technology Co., Ltd was the majority shareholder of Allestari Development Pte Ltd until 2011.

On 2008-07-01, Allestari Development Pte Ltd adopted articles including Article 76A by special resolution, providing that Allway Minerals and Science Technology Co., Ltd was entitled to appoint and remove three members of the board of directors, each of Squad 308 and the BVI Holdco was entitled to appoint and remove one member of the board of directors, and there would be one independent member of the board whose nomination required approval by the board including an affirmative vote from the Investor Director.
In 2008, Allway Minerals and Science Technology Co., Ltd appointed three individuals, including Li Yi, as directors of Allestari Development Pte Ltd pursuant to Article 76A.

After Allway Minerals and Science Technology Co., Ltd ceased to be a shareholder of Allestari Development Pte Ltd in 2011, the three directors originally appointed by Allway remained on the board and were not removed by the shareholders.

Allestari Development Pte Ltd has a memorandum and articles of association dated 2006-12-11, referred to as its Constitution, which includes provisions on nomination, appointment and removal of directors such as Articles 76A and 86 to 88.

Articles 86 to 88 of Allestari Development Pte Ltd's Constitution provide that, except with respect to the Investor Director, the company may by ordinary resolution remove any director and appoint another person, and may appoint new directors by ordinary resolution or by decision of the existing directors, Article 76A.

The Companies Act 1967 (2020 Rev Ed) provides default rules applicable to Allestari Development Pte Ltd, including section 149B on appointment of directors by ordinary resolution where the constitution does not otherwise provide, and section 152(9) on removal of directors of a private company by ordinary resolution the constitution.

On 2022-11-25, in HC/OC 798/2023, an interim injunction was granted on the application of N Century Holding Company Limited restraining Clarity China Partners LP and Allestari Development Pte Ltd from altering the composition of Allestari's board, including by removal or substitution of any director.

N Century Holding Company Limited applied for the interim injunction in HC/OC 798/2023 in response to an attempt by Clarity China Partners LP to remove three directors from the board of Allestari Development Pte Ltd, namely Liu Xun, Guo Jian and Li Yi.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether the existing constitutional provisions of Allestari Development Pte Ltd and the interim injunction in HC/OC 798/2023 prevented court-ordered changes to the board composition sought by a minority shareholder through implied terms and mandatory orders.
Party_Details.Rule: A company’s board composition is ordinarily determined by its constitution and applicable statutory provisions, including rules that directors may be appointed and removed by ordinary resolution unless the constitution provides otherwise. Courts generally do not override shareholders’ rights to choose board members who meet any qualification requirements in the constitution, and declaratory or mandatory relief affecting board composition is typically granted only where there is a real controversy and a proper legal basis consistent with the company’s constitutional and statutory framework.
Party_Details.Application: Allestari Development Pte Ltd had adopted Article 76A in 2008, granting specific director appointment rights to named shareholders, and its constitution and the Companies Act provided mechanisms for appointment and removal of directors by ordinary resolution. An interim injunction in HC/OC 798/2023, obtained in response to an attempt by a majority shareholder to remove three directors, restrained changes to the board composition. Against this background, a minority shareholder sought court orders to imply new terms into the constitution reallocating appointment rights and to require steps to remove and replace certain directors. These requests intersected with the existing constitutional scheme and the injunction that preserved the current board, and would have altered how Allestari’s shareholders exercise their existing rights to appoint and remove directors.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The judge held that the court would not interfere with shareholders’ rights to choose board members so long as the board members meet any qualification requirements in the company’s constitution. The existing constitution of Allestari Development Pte Ltd, including Article 76A and Articles 86 to 88, together with the Companies Act, already provided a framework for appointment and removal of directors by ordinary resolution. The interim injunction in HC/OC 798/2023 preserved the existing board composition while a dispute between major shareholders over shareholdings was being resolved. The judge concluded that the relief sought by the minority shareholder would effectively bypass these mechanisms and the injunction, and that there was no legal necessity or live controversy justifying the court in imposing new implied terms or mandatory orders that would reallocate director appointment rights or compel removal of directors.

Metadata.Case_Number: Originating Application No 1153 of 2024 (Registrar’s Appeal No 13 of 2025)
Metadata.Tribunal_Court: General Division of the High Court of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: N Century Holding Company Limited

Facts.Text: According to the register of Allestari Development Pte Ltd, N Century Holding Company Limited holds 20.28% of the shares in Allestari Development Pte Ltd, with its relative shareholding compared to Clarity China Partners LP being the subject of dispute in HC/OC 798/2023.

On 2022-11-25, N Century Holding Company Limited applied in HC/OC 798/2023 for an interim injunction restraining Clarity China Partners LP and Allestari Development Pte Ltd from altering the composition of Allestari's board, including by removal or substitution of any director.

N Century Holding Company Limited's application for the interim injunction in HC/OC 798/2023 was made in response to an attempt by Clarity China Partners LP to remove three directors from the board of Allestari Development Pte Ltd, namely Liu Xun, Guo Jian and Li Yi.

N Century Holding Company Limited applied to strike out Originating Application No 1153 of 2024 in its entirety under Rules of Court 2021 Order 9 Rule 16(1), contending that the claim was time-barred, factually and legally unsustainable, and an abuse of process.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether N Century Holding Company Limited, as a substantial shareholder of Allestari Development Pte Ltd and applicant for the interim injunction in HC/OC 798/2023, could successfully seek to strike out the minority shareholder’s originating application that sought to affect the board composition and implied terms in the constitution.
Party_Details.Rule: A party may apply to strike out a claim under procedural rules where the claim discloses no reasonable cause of action, is legally or factually unsustainable, or constitutes an abuse of process. Courts may consider whether the relief sought conflicts with existing injunctions, whether there is a real controversy, and whether the pleaded legal basis can succeed on the facts advanced.
Party_Details.Application: N Century Holding Company Limited, holding approximately 20.28% of Allestari Development Pte Ltd, had previously obtained an interim injunction preserving the board composition in HC/OC 798/2023 after an attempt by another shareholder to remove three directors. When a minority shareholder later filed Originating Application No 1153 of 2024 seeking to imply terms into the constitution and to require steps to remove and replace the same three directors, N Century applied to strike out the proceedings. N Century argued that the application conflicted with the existing injunction, lacked a sustainable legal and factual basis for the implied terms and relief sought, and amounted to an abuse of process. The relief requested in the originating application, if granted, would have affected N Century’s position as a shareholder and the board composition that the injunction was designed to preserve.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The judge accepted N Century Holding Company Limited’s position that the originating application should be struck out. The judge agreed that the proceedings were an abuse of process because they operated as a collateral attack on the interim injunction obtained by N Century in HC/OC 798/2023, which restrained changes to the board composition of Allestari Development Pte Ltd. The judge also agreed with N Century that the claim for implication of terms into the constitution was legally and factually unsustainable under the established test for implied terms, and that there was no live controversy warranting declaratory relief. The judge noted N Century’s concern that if the action were not struck out against all defendants, a default judgment against another defendant could adversely affect N Century, and concluded that the grounds for striking out applied to the action as a whole. The judge therefore upheld the assistant registrar’s decision to strike out the entire action and awarded N Century costs of $12,000 all-in, explaining that preparation for an appeal requires counsel to be ready to address all issues in a rehearing.
